---
title: "Parenthood, Immigration and Further Education"
subtitle: "Analyses and Visualizations"
author: "Nate Breznau, Johannes Bonnes and Sylvia Rahn"
output:
  word_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: '1'
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    number_sections: true
    toc_depth: 1
  pdf_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: '1'
---


## Setup   

```{r setup, echo = F, warning = F, message = F}

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)

packages <- c('tidyverse',
              'readr',
              'knitr',
              'here',
              'ggplot2',
              'ggrepel',
              'ragg',
              'kableExtra',
              'countrycode',
              'readxl',
              'jtools',
              'lme4',
              'lmerTest',
              'broom.mixed',
              'viridis',
              'survey',
              'ggpubr',
              'purrr')

pacman::p_load(packages, character.only = T)

```

## Data

```{r }


df <- read_rds(here("Data", "df.RDS")) 
df2 <- read_rds(here("Data", "df2.RDS")) 

```

## Regressions

### Table 1. OLS



```{r}
df_imm <- subset(df2, FORBORN == 1 & !is.na(NFE12) & !is.na(children) & !is.na(FORLANG) & !is.na(ed3) & ed_currently != 1 & !is.na(female))

df_nat <- subset(df2, FORBORN == 0 & !is.na(NFE12) & !is.na(children) & !is.na(FORLANG) & !is.na(ed3) & ed_currently != 1 & !is.na(female))

df_clean <- subset(df2, !is.na(NFE12) & !is.na(children) & !is.na(FORLANG) & !is.na(ed3) & ed_currently != 1 & !is.na(female))

M1.1 <- lm(skill_reading ~ NFE12 + female + children + FORLANG + factor(ed3) + factor(iso3c), data = df_imm)

M1.2 <- lm(skill_reading ~ NFE12 + female + children + FORLANG + factor(ed3) + factor(iso3c), data = df_nat)

M1.3 <- lm(skill_reading ~ NFE12 + female + children + FORLANG + FORBORN + factor(ed3) + factor(iso3c), data = df_clean)

M1.4 <- lm(skill_reading ~ NFE12 + female + children + FORLANG + FORBORN*NFE12 + factor(ed3) + factor(iso3c), data = df_clean)

export_summs(
  M1.1, M1.2, M1.3, M1.4,
  model.names = c("M1.1", "M1.2", "M1.3", "M1.4"),
  coefs = c(
    "(Intercept)",
    "NFE12",
    "female",
    "children",
    "FORLANG",
    "FORBORN",
    "factor(ed3)2",
    "factor(ed3)3",
    "NFE12:FORBORN"
  ),
  statistics = c("N" = "nobs", "r2" = "r.squared", "AIC" = "AIC", "BIC" = "BIC"),
  to.file = "docx",
  file.name = here("Results", "Reg_Table_1.docx")
)

```
### Sample Bias Test

Here I correlate the residuals with the weighting variable. If this is close to zero I can look at the sample as a representative treatment for ALE and literacy. 

```{r}

df_imm$resid_imm_1 <- residuals(M1.1)
df_nat$resid_nat_1 <- residuals(M1.2)
df_clean$resid_clean <- residuals(M1.3)

testcor1 <- cor(df_imm$aweight, df_imm$resid_imm_1)
testcor2 <- cor(df_nat$aweight, df_nat$resid_nat_1)
testcor3 <- cor(df_clean$aweight, df_clean$resid_clean)

testp1 <- cor.test(df_imm$aweight, df_imm$resid_imm_1)$p.value
testp2 <- cor.test(df_nat$aweight, df_nat$resid_nat_1)$p.value
testp3 <- cor.test(df_clean$aweight, df_clean$resid_clean)$p.value

```

Correlations are `r round(testcor1,3)`, `r round(testcor2,3)`, & `r round(testcor3,3)`. With p-values `r round(testp1,4)`, `r round(testp2,4)`, & `r round(testp3,4)`. This suggests that there is no need to weight the sample. We are essentially controlling for the variables that lead to selection.

### Table 2. GLM/Multilevel

#### Recodes

```{r}
library(dplyr)

df2_sub <- df2 %>%
  mutate(
    ed3_secondary = as.integer(ed3 == 2),
    ed3_tertiary  = as.integer(ed3 == 3)
  ) %>%
  subset(ed_currently != 1)

# 1) within/between decomposition (weighted country means)
df2_sub <- df2_sub %>%
  group_by(iso3c) %>%
  mutate(
    male = ifelse(female == 1, 0, 1),
    NFE12_between = sum(SPFWT0 * NFE12, na.rm = TRUE) / sum(SPFWT0[!is.na(NFE12)], na.rm = TRUE),
    female_between = sum(SPFWT0 * female, na.rm = TRUE) / sum(SPFWT0[!is.na(female)], na.rm = TRUE),
    male_between = sum(SPFWT0 * male, na.rm = T) / sum(SPFWT0[!is.na(male)], na.rm = TRUE),
    children_between = sum(SPFWT0 * children, na.rm = TRUE) / sum(SPFWT0[!is.na(children)], na.rm = TRUE),
    ed3_secondary_between = sum(SPFWT0 * ed3_secondary, na.rm = TRUE) / sum(SPFWT0[!is.na(ed3_secondary)], na.rm = TRUE),
    ed3_tertiary_between  = sum(SPFWT0 * ed3_tertiary,  na.rm = TRUE) / sum(SPFWT0[!is.na(ed3_tertiary)],  na.rm = TRUE),

    forborn_between = sum(SPFWT0 * FORBORN, na.rm = TRUE) / sum(SPFWT0[!is.na(FORBORN)], na.rm = TRUE),
    forlang_between = sum(SPFWT0 * FORLANG, na.rm = TRUE) / sum(SPFWT0[!is.na(FORLANG)], na.rm = TRUE),

    NFE12_within = NFE12 - NFE12_between,
    children_within = children - children_between,
    female_within = female - female_between,
    ed3_secondary_within = ed3_secondary - ed3_secondary_between,
    ed3_tertiary_within = ed3_tertiary - ed3_tertiary_between,
    forborn_within = FORBORN - forborn_between,
    forlang_within = FORLANG - forlang_between
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# the within effects need to be further centered by group 

df2_imm <- df2_sub %>% 
  subset(FORBORN == 1) %>% 
  group_by(iso3c) %>% 
  mutate(NFE12_within = NFE12_within - mean(NFE12_within, na.rm = TRUE), 
         female_within = female_within - mean(female_within, na.rm = T), 
         children_within = children_within - mean(children_within, na.rm = TRUE), 
         ed3_secondary_within = ed3_secondary_within - mean(ed3_secondary_within, na.rm = TRUE), 
         ed3_tertiary_within = ed3_tertiary_within - mean(ed3_tertiary_within, na.rm = TRUE) ) %>% 
  ungroup() 


df2_nat <- df2_sub %>% 
  subset(FORBORN_not == 1) %>% 
  group_by(iso3c) %>% 
  mutate(NFE12_within = NFE12_within - mean(NFE12_within, na.rm = TRUE),
         children_within = children_within - mean(children_within, na.rm = TRUE), 
         female_within = female_within - mean(female_within, na.rm = T), 
         ed3_secondary_within = ed3_secondary_within - mean(ed3_secondary_within, na.rm = TRUE), 
          ed3_tertiary_within = ed3_tertiary_within - mean(ed3_tertiary_within, na.rm = TRUE) ) %>% ungroup()

between_vars <- c(
  "NFE12_between", "female_between", "children_between",
  "ed3_secondary_between", "ed3_tertiary_between",
  "forborn_between", "forlang_between"
)

# build country-level x-standardized-scored between 
country_between_scaled <- function(dat, between_vars, suffix = "_z") {

  # get one row per country (so no implicit weighting by country sample size)
  country_vals <- dat %>%
    distinct(iso3c, across(all_of(between_vars)))

  # z-score across countries
  country_z <- country_vals %>%
    mutate(
      across(all_of(between_vars), ~{
        m <- mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE)
        s <- sd(.x, na.rm = TRUE)
        if (is.na(s) || s == 0) NA_real_ else as.numeric((.x - m) / s)
      }, .names = paste0("{.col}", suffix))
    ) %>%
    select(iso3c, ends_with(suffix))

  country_z
}
```


```{r}

# df2
df2_sub <- df2_sub %>%
  left_join(country_between_scaled(df2_sub, between_vars), by = "iso3c")

# df2_imm
df2_imm <- df2_imm %>%
  left_join(country_between_scaled(df2_imm, between_vars), by = "iso3c") 

# df2_nat
df2_nat <- df2_nat %>%
  left_join(country_between_scaled(df2_nat, between_vars), by = "iso3c") 
```


#### Models

I first look at immigrants and native-born separately. Then combine them to determine if there is an 'immigrant penalty' after controlling for participation in further education and other variables. 

```{r}

M2.1 <- lmer(
  skill_reading ~ 
    NFE12_within + NFE12_between_z +
    female_within + female_between_z +
    children_within + children_between_z +
    forlang_within + forlang_between_z +
    ed3_secondary_within + ed3_secondary_between_z +
    ed3_tertiary_within + ed3_tertiary_between_z +
    (1 | iso3c),
  data = df2_imm
)

ranef_list <- ranef(M2.1)

# Get country-level grand intercept deviations
iso3c_intercepts <- ranef_list$iso3c



M2.2 <- lmer(
  skill_reading ~ 
    NFE12_within + NFE12_between_z +
    female_within + female_between_z +
    children_within + children_between_z +
    forlang_within + forlang_between_z +
    ed3_secondary_within + ed3_secondary_between_z +
    ed3_tertiary_within + ed3_tertiary_between_z +
    (1 | iso3c),
  data = df2_nat
)

M2.3a <- lmer(
  skill_reading ~ 
    NFE12_within + NFE12_between_z +
    children_within + children_between_z +
    female_within + female_between_z +
    forlang_within + forlang_between_z +
    forborn_within + forborn_between_z +
    ed3_secondary_within + ed3_secondary_between_z +
    ed3_tertiary_within + ed3_tertiary_between_z +
    (1 | iso3c),
  data = subset(df2_sub)
)

M2.3b <- lmer(
  skill_reading ~ 
    NFE12_within + NFE12_between_z +
    children_within + children_between_z +
    female_within + female_between_z +
    forlang_within + forlang_between_z +
    forborn_within + forborn_between_z +
    ed3_secondary_within + ed3_secondary_between_z +
    ed3_tertiary_within + ed3_tertiary_between_z +
    gdppc_k +
    (1 | iso3c),
  data = subset(df2_sub)
)

M2.4 <- lmer(
  skill_reading ~ 
    NFE12_within + NFE12_between_z +
    children_within + children_between_z +
    female_within + female_between_z +
    forlang_within + forlang_between_z +
    forborn_within + forborn_between_z +
    forborn_within*NFE12_within +
    ed3_secondary_within + ed3_secondary_between_z +
    ed3_tertiary_within + ed3_tertiary_between_z +
    (1 | iso3c),
  data = subset(df2_sub)
)

```

#### Results


```{r}

export_summs(
  M2.1, M2.2, M2.3a, M2.3b, M2.4,
  #model.names = c("Foreign-born", "Native-born", "Pooled", "Pooled with ALE interaction"),
  to.file = "docx",
  file.name = here("Results", "Reg_Table_2.docx"
))

```

#### GDP?

The correlation is significant at p <0.1 without the US. The US is kind of a disaster in this figure. 

```{r}
test <- df2_sub %>%
    group_by(iso3c) %>%
    summarise(lit = first(skill_reading), gdp = first(gdppc_k))

test_nousa <- subset(test, iso3c != "USA")

ct <- cor.test(test$gdp, test$lit)
ctusa <- cor.test(test_nousa$gdp, test_nousa$lit)

lab <- sprintf("r = %.2f, p = %.3f", ct$estimate, ct$p.value)
labusa <- sprintf("r = %.2f, p = %.3f", ctusa$estimate, ctusa$p.value)

ggplot(test, aes(gdp, lit)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  annotate("text",
           x = Inf, y = Inf,
           label = lab,
           hjust = 1.1, vjust = 1.5)

# without the US
ggplot(test_nousa, aes(gdp, lit)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  annotate("text",
           x = Inf, y = Inf,
           label = labusa,
           hjust = 1.1, vjust = 1.5)

```


### ATEs

### Table S2. Interactive GLM

Here we interact all intersectional variables of interest with ALE (NFE12) to see the unique effects if any.

The correlation between foreign-born and foreign-language is not so high as to warrant dropping one.

select(df2, FORBORN, FORLANG) %>% cor(., use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
          FORBORN   FORLANG
FORBORN 1.0000000 0.5645113
FORLANG 0.5645113 1.0000000

```{r}
m4 <- lmer(
  skill_reading ~ 
    NFE12_within + NFE12_between_z +
    female_within + female_between_z + 
    children_within + children_between_z +
    forlang_within + forlang_between_z +
    forborn_within + forborn_between_z +
    # interaction with all sub-interactions
    female_within*forborn_within*children_within*forlang_within*NFE12_within +
    ed3_secondary_within + ed3_secondary_between_z +
    ed3_tertiary_within + ed3_tertiary_between_z +
    gdppc_k_log +
    (1 | iso3c),
  data = subset(df2_sub)
)


export_summs(
  m4,
  coefs = c(
    "(Intercept)" = "(Intercept)",
    "ALE (within)" = "NFE12_within",
    "ALE (between)" = "NFE12_between_z",
    "Female (within)" = "female_within",
    "Female (between)" = "female_between_z",
    "Children (within)" = "children_within",
    "Children (between)" = "children_between_z",
    "Non-native speaker (within)" = "forlang_within",
    "Non-native speaker (between)" = "forlang_between_z",
    "Foreign-born (within)" = "forborn_within",
    "Foreign-born (between)" = "forborn_between_z",
    "Secondary education (within)" = "ed3_secondary_within",
    "Secondary education (between)" = "ed3_secondary_between_z",
    "Tertiary education (within)" = "ed3_tertiary_within",
    "Tertiary education (between)" = "ed3_tertiary_between_z",
    "GDP per capita (log k)" = "gdppc_k_log",
    "Female x Foreign-born (within)" = "female_within:forborn_within",
    "Female x Children (within)" = "female_within:children_within",
    "Children x Foreign-born (within)" = "children_within:forborn_within",      
    "Female x Foreign-language (within)" = "female_within:forlang_within",  
    "Foreign-language x Foreign-born (within)" = "forlang_within:forborn_within", 
    "Children x Foreign-language (within)" = "children_within:forlang_within",
    "ALE x Female (within)" =  "NFE12_within:female_within",        
    "ALE x Foreign-born (within)" = "NFE12_within:forborn_within",
    "ALE x Children (within)" = "NFE12_within:children_within",                 
    "ALE x Foreign-language (within)" = "NFE12_within:forlang_within",    
    "Female x Children x Foreign-born" = "female_within:children_within:forborn_within",  
    "Female x Foreign-language x Foreign-born (within)" = "female_within:forlang_within:forborn_within",  
    "Female x Children x Foreign-language (within)" = "female_within:children_within:forlang_within",
    "Children x Foreign-language x Foreign-born (within)" = "children_within:forlang_within:forborn_within",
    "ALE x Female x Foreign-born (within)" = "NFE12_within:female_within:forborn_within", 
    "ALE x Female x Children (within)" = "NFE12_within:female_within:children_within",
    "ALE x Children x Foreign-born (within)" = "NFE12_within:children_within:forborn_within",  
    "ALE x Female x Foreign-language (within)" = "NFE12_within:female_within:forlang_within",  
    "ALE x Foreign-language x Foreign-born (within)" = "NFE12_within:forlang_within:forborn_within",
    "ALE x Children x Foreign-language (within)" = "NFE12_within:children_within:forlang_within",
    "Female x Children x Foreign-language x Foreign-born (within)" = "female_within:children_within:forlang_within:forborn_within", 
    "ALE x Female x Children x Foreign-born (within)" = "NFE12_within:female_within:children_within:forborn_within",    
    "ALE x Female x Foreign-language x Foreign-born (within)" = "NFE12_within:female_within:forlang_within:forborn_within",
    "ALE x Female x Children x Foreign-language (within)" = "NFE12_within:female_within:children_within:forlang_within",   
    "ALE x Children x Foreign-language x Foreign-born" = "NFE12_within:children_within:forlang_within:forborn_within",
    "ALE x Female x Children x Foreign-language x Foreign-born" = "NFE12_within:female_within:children_within:forlang_within:forborn_within"
  ),
  to.file = "docx",
  file.name = here("Results", "Table_S3.docx")
)

include_graphics(here("Results", "Table_S3.docx"))
```
#### Predictions

```{r}

df2r <- subset(df2_sub, !is.na(female) & !is.na(NFE12) & !is.na(children_within) & !is.na(forborn_within) & !is.na(forlang_within) & !is.na(ed3) & !is.na(gdppc) & !is.na(skill_reading))

# Calculate grand means. This is necessary because of the centering. The between effect would otherwise create artifact effects because there are more or less immigrants (or whatever group) in a country

gm_female <- mean(df2r$female_between, na.rm = TRUE)
gm_forborn <- mean(df2r$forborn_between, na.rm = TRUE)
gm_children <- mean(df2r$children_between, na.rm = TRUE)
gm_forlang <- mean(df2r$forlang_between, na.rm = TRUE)
gm_nfe12 <- mean(df2r$NFE12_between, na.rm = TRUE)

#Type 1: Foreign-born parent, non-native speaker
df_type10 <- df2r %>% mutate(female_within = 1 - gm_female, forborn_within = 1 - gm_forborn, children_within = 1 - gm_children, forlang_within = 1 - gm_forlang, NFE12_within = 0 - gm_nfe12)
df_type11 <- df2r %>% mutate(female_within = 1 - gm_female, forborn_within = 1 - gm_forborn, children_within = 1 - gm_children, forlang_within = 1 - gm_forlang, NFE12_within = 1 - gm_nfe12)
df_type10m <- df2r %>% mutate(female_within = 0 - gm_female, forborn_within = 1 - gm_forborn, children_within = 1 - gm_children, forlang_within = 1 - gm_forlang, NFE12_within = 0 - gm_nfe12)
df_type11m <- df2r %>% mutate(female_within = 0 - gm_female, forborn_within = 1 - gm_forborn, children_within = 1 - gm_children, forlang_within = 1 - gm_forlang, NFE12_within = 1 - gm_nfe12)

#Type 2: Foreign-born no children, non-native speaker
df_type20 <- df2r %>% mutate(female_within = 1 - gm_female, forborn_within = 1 - gm_forborn, children_within = 0 - gm_children, forlang_within = 1 - gm_forlang, NFE12_within = 0 - gm_nfe12)
df_type21 <- df2r %>% mutate(female_within = 1 - gm_female, forborn_within = 1 - gm_forborn, children_within = 0 - gm_children, forlang_within = 1 - gm_forlang, NFE12_within = 1 - gm_nfe12)
df_type20m <- df2r %>% mutate(female_within = 0 - gm_female, forborn_within = 1 - gm_forborn, children_within = 0 - gm_children, forlang_within = 1 - gm_forlang, NFE12_within = 0 - gm_nfe12)
df_type21m <- df2r %>% mutate(female_within = 0 - gm_female, forborn_within = 1 - gm_forborn, children_within = 0 - gm_children, forlang_within = 1 - gm_forlang, NFE12_within = 1 - gm_nfe12)

#Type 3: Foreign-born parent, native speaker
df_type30 <- df2r %>% mutate(female_within = 1 - gm_female, forborn_within = 1 - gm_forborn, children_within = 1 - gm_children, forlang_within = 0 - gm_forlang, NFE12_within = 0 - gm_nfe12)
df_type31 <- df2r %>% mutate(female_within = 1 - gm_female, forborn_within = 1 - gm_forborn, children_within = 1 - gm_children, forlang_within = 0 - gm_forlang, NFE12_within = 1 - gm_nfe12)
df_type30m <- df2r %>% mutate(female_within = 0 - gm_female, forborn_within = 1 - gm_forborn, children_within = 1 - gm_children, forlang_within = 0 - gm_forlang, NFE12_within = 0 - gm_nfe12)
df_type31m <- df2r %>% mutate(female_within = 0 - gm_female, forborn_within = 1 - gm_forborn, children_within = 1 - gm_children, forlang_within = 0 - gm_forlang, NFE12_within = 1 - gm_nfe12)

#Type 4: Foreign-born no children, native speaker
df_type40 <- df2r %>% mutate(female_within = 1 - gm_female, forborn_within = 1 - gm_forborn, children_within = 0 - gm_children, forlang_within = 0 - gm_forlang, NFE12_within = 0 - gm_nfe12)
df_type41 <- df2r %>% mutate(female_within = 1 - gm_female, forborn_within = 1 - gm_forborn, children_within = 0 - gm_children, forlang_within = 0 - gm_forlang, NFE12_within = 1 - gm_nfe12)
df_type40m <- df2r %>% mutate(female_within = 0 - gm_female, forborn_within = 1 - gm_forborn, children_within = 0 - gm_children, forlang_within = 0 - gm_forlang, NFE12_within = 0 - gm_nfe12)
df_type41m <- df2r %>% mutate(female_within = 0 - gm_female, forborn_within = 1 - gm_forborn, children_within = 0 - gm_children, forlang_within = 0 - gm_forlang, NFE12_within = 1 - gm_nfe12)

#Type 5: Native-born parent, native speaker
df_type50 <- df2r %>% mutate(female_within = 1 - gm_female, forborn_within = 0 - gm_forborn, children_within = 1 - gm_children, forlang_within = 0 - gm_forlang, NFE12_within = 0 - gm_nfe12)
df_type51 <- df2r %>% mutate(female_within = 1 - gm_female, forborn_within = 0 - gm_forborn, children_within = 1 - gm_children, forlang_within = 0 - gm_forlang, NFE12_within = 1 - gm_nfe12)
df_type50m <- df2r %>% mutate(female_within = 0 - gm_female, forborn_within = 0 - gm_forborn, children_within = 1 - gm_children, forlang_within = 0 - gm_forlang, NFE12_within = 0 - gm_nfe12)
df_type51m <- df2r %>% mutate(female_within = 0 - gm_female, forborn_within = 0 - gm_forborn, children_within = 1 - gm_children, forlang_within = 0 - gm_forlang, NFE12_within = 1 - gm_nfe12)

#Type 6: Native-born no children, native speaker
df_type60 <- df2r %>% mutate(female_within = 1 - gm_female, forborn_within = 0 - gm_forborn, children_within = 0 - gm_children, forlang_within = 0 - gm_forlang, NFE12_within = 0 - gm_nfe12)
df_type61 <- df2r %>% mutate(female_within = 1 - gm_female, forborn_within = 0 - gm_forborn, children_within = 0 - gm_children, forlang_within = 0 - gm_forlang, NFE12_within = 1 - gm_nfe12)
df_type60m <- df2r %>% mutate(female_within = 0 - gm_female, forborn_within = 0 - gm_forborn, children_within = 0 - gm_children, forlang_within = 0 - gm_forlang, NFE12_within = 0 - gm_nfe12)
df_type61m <- df2r %>% mutate(female_within = 0 - gm_female, forborn_within = 0 - gm_forborn, children_within = 0 - gm_children, forlang_within = 0 - gm_forlang, NFE12_within = 1 - gm_nfe12)

# make predictions

pred10 <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type10, re.form = NA)
pred11 <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type11, re.form = NA)
pred10m <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type10m, re.form = NA)
pred11m <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type11m, re.form = NA)
pred20 <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type20, re.form = NA)
pred21 <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type21, re.form = NA)
pred20m <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type20m, re.form = NA)
pred21m <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type21m, re.form = NA)
pred30 <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type30, re.form = NA)
pred31 <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type31, re.form = NA)
pred30m <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type30m, re.form = NA)
pred31m <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type31m, re.form = NA)
pred40 <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type40, re.form = NA)
pred41 <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type41, re.form = NA)
pred40m <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type40m, re.form = NA)
pred41m <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type41m, re.form = NA)
pred50 <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type50, re.form = NA)
pred51 <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type51, re.form = NA)
pred50m <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type50m, re.form = NA)
pred51m <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type51m, re.form = NA)
pred60 <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type60, re.form = NA)
pred61 <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type61, re.form = NA)
pred60m <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type60m, re.form = NA)
pred61m <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type61m, re.form = NA)


# Calculate average marginal effect

diff1 <- pred10 - pred11
diff2 <- pred20 - pred21
diff3 <- pred30 - pred31
diff4 <- pred40 - pred41
diff5 <- pred50 - pred51
diff6 <- pred60 - pred61

diff1m <- pred10m - pred11m
diff2m <- pred20m - pred21m
diff3m <- pred30m - pred31m
diff4m <- pred40m - pred41m
diff5m <- pred50m - pred51m
diff6m <- pred60m - pred61m




AME1 <- mean(diff1)
ttest1 <- t.test(pred11, pred10, paired = FALSE)
AME2 <- mean(diff2)
ttest2 <- t.test(pred21, pred20, paired = FALSE)
AME3 <- mean(diff3)
ttest3 <- t.test(pred31, pred30, paired = FALSE)
AME4 <- mean(diff4)
ttest4 <- t.test(pred41, pred40, paired = FALSE)
AME5 <- mean(diff5)
ttest5 <- t.test(pred51, pred50, paired = FALSE)
AME6 <- mean(diff6)
ttest6 <- t.test(pred61, pred60, paired = FALSE)

AME1m <- mean(diff1m)
ttest1m <- t.test(pred11m, pred10m, paired = FALSE)
AME2m <- mean(diff2m)
ttest2m <- t.test(pred21m, pred20m, paired = FALSE)
AME3m <- mean(diff3m)
ttest3m <- t.test(pred31m, pred30m, paired = FALSE)
AME4m <- mean(diff4m)
ttest4m <- t.test(pred41m, pred40m, paired = FALSE)
AME5m <- mean(diff5m)
ttest5m <- t.test(pred51m, pred50m, paired = FALSE)
AME6m <- mean(diff6m)
ttest6m <- t.test(pred61m, pred60m, paired = FALSE)


```


#### Plot

##### Combine values

```{r}



ame_df <- data.frame(
  type = c(
    "1f. Foreign-born mother\nnon-native speaker",
    "2f. Foreign-born no children female\nnon-native speaker",
    "3f. Foreign-born mother\nnative speaker",
    "4f. Foreign-born no children female\nnative speaker",
    "5f. Native-born mother\nnative speaker",
    "6f. Native-born no children female\nnative speaker"
  ),
  AME = c(AME1, AME2, AME3, AME4, AME5, AME6),
  lower = c(ttest1$conf.int[1], ttest2$conf.int[1], ttest3$conf.int[1],
            ttest4$conf.int[1], ttest5$conf.int[1], ttest6$conf.int[1]),
  upper = c(ttest1$conf.int[2], ttest2$conf.int[2], ttest3$conf.int[2],
            ttest4$conf.int[2], ttest5$conf.int[2], ttest6$conf.int[2])
)

ame_df <- ame_df %>%
  mutate(
    AME = -AME,
    lower = lower,
    upper = upper
  )

ame_dfm <- data.frame(
  type = c(
    "1m. Foreign-born father\nnon-native speaker",
    "2m. Foreign-born no children male\nnon-native speaker",
    "3m. Foreign-born father\nnative speaker",
    "4m. Foreign-born no children male\nnative speaker",
    "5m. Native-born father\nnative speaker",
    "6m. Native-born no children male\nnative speaker"
  ),
  AME = c(AME1m, AME2m, AME3m, AME4m, AME5m, AME6m),
  lower = c(ttest1m$conf.int[1], ttest2m$conf.int[1], ttest3m$conf.int[1],
            ttest4m$conf.int[1], ttest5m$conf.int[1], ttest6m$conf.int[1]),
  upper = c(ttest1m$conf.int[2], ttest2m$conf.int[2], ttest3m$conf.int[2],
            ttest4m$conf.int[2], ttest5m$conf.int[2], ttest6m$conf.int[2])
)

ame_dfm <- ame_dfm %>%
  mutate(
    AME = -AME,
    lower = lower,
    upper = upper
  )

ame_all <- bind_rows(
  ame_df  %>% mutate(sex = "f"),
  ame_dfm %>% mutate(sex = "m")
)


ame_all <- ame_all %>%
  mutate(type = factor(type, levels = rev(type)))

pal12 <- viridis(n = nlevels(ame_all$type), option = "D", begin = 0.1, end = 0.9)

# extract numeric prefix (1–6) and sex (f/m)
ame_all <- ame_all %>%
  mutate(
    idx = as.integer(substr(type, 1, 1)),
    sex = ifelse(grepl("^\\dm\\.", type), "m", "f")
  )

# build desired order: 1f,1m,2f,2m,...
type_order <- ame_all %>%
  distinct(type, idx, sex) %>%
  arrange(idx, sex) %>%          # f comes before m alphabetically
  pull(type)

# apply order (reverse because coord_flip)
ame_all$type <- factor(ame_all$type, levels = rev(type_order))

```

##### Fig 1 Old Style orig CIs

```{r}

agg_png(here("Results", "Fig1.png"), res = 144, height = 750, width = 750)

ggplot(ame_all, aes(x = type, y = AME, fill = type)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.55, color = "black") +              # thinner bars
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), width = 0.15) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal12) +                    # 12 viridis colors
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Average Treatment Effect\nof Participation in ALE",
    fill = "Type"
  ) +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y  = element_text(hjust = 1, size = 8),
    axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 8),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 8),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

dev.off()

include_graphics(here("Results", "Fig1.png"))
```
### Fig 1. New Label


```{r}



ame_all <- bind_rows(
  ame_df  %>% mutate(sex = "f"),
  ame_dfm %>% mutate(sex = "m")
) %>%
  mutate(
    idx = as.integer(substr(type, 1, 1)),
    sex = ifelse(grepl("^\\dm\\.", type), "m", "f")
  )


type_order <- ame_all %>%
  distinct(type, idx, sex) %>%
  arrange(idx, sex) %>%
  pull(type)

ame_all <- ame_all %>%
  mutate(
    type = factor(type, levels = rev(type_order))  # reverse for top-to-bottom display
  )

# Add binary indicators for the dot matrix

ame_all <- ame_all %>%
  mutate(
    Female = ifelse(sex == "f", 1, 0),
    `Foreign-born` = case_when(
      idx %in% c(1, 2, 3, 4) ~ 1,
      idx %in% c(5, 6) ~ 0
    ),
    `Non-native-speaker` = case_when(
      idx %in% c(1, 2) ~ 1,
      idx %in% c(3, 4, 5, 6) ~ 0
    ),
    Parent = case_when(
      idx %in% c(1, 3, 5) ~ 1,
      idx %in% c(2, 4, 6) ~ 0
    )
  )

# Convert for dots

dot_df <- ame_all %>%
  select(type, Female, `Foreign-born`, `Non-native-speaker`, Parent) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(Female, `Foreign-born`, `Non-native-speaker`, Parent),
    names_to = "variable",
    values_to = "value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    variable = factor(
      variable,
      levels = c("Female", "Foreign-born", "Non-native-speaker", "Parent")
    )
  )

# New labelling with dots in a matrix

p_dots <- ggplot(dot_df, aes(x = variable, y = type)) +
  geom_point(
    data = subset(dot_df, value == 0),
    shape = 21,
    size = 3.2,
    stroke = 0.5,
    fill = "white",
    color = "black"
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = subset(dot_df, value == 1),
    shape = 16,
    size = 3.2,
    color = "black"
  ) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10, angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )


p_bar <- ggplot(ame_all, aes(x = type, y = AME, fill = type)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.55, color = "black") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), width = 0.15) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal12) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 60, by = 5)) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Average Treatment Effect\nof Participation in ALE"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

# Coombine panels

#p_final <- p_dots + p_bar +
#  plot_layout(widths = c(1.1, 2.4))

agg_png(here("Results", "Fig1_dotmatrix.png"), res = 144, height = 750, width = 1100)

ggarrange(
  p_dots,
  p_bar,
  ncol = 2,
  widths = c(1.1, 2.4),
  align = "h"
)

dev.off()

include_graphics(here("Results", "Fig1_dotmatrix.png"))

```


##### Adjust CIs

The confidence intervals here refer to the entire sample across 26 countries. To make them more realistic to a treatment in one country, we adjust them down to a sample of 5,700 which is the average sample size per country.

```{r}

# Function to adjust CI to a new sample size (e.g., average country = 5700)
adjust_ci <- function(ttest_obj, new_n = 5700) {
  mean_diff <- ttest_obj$estimate[1] - ttest_obj$estimate[2]
  pooled_sd <- sqrt(((ttest_obj$parameter + 1) * ttest_obj$stderr^2))  # back-calculate SD
  se_new <- pooled_sd / sqrt(new_n)
  ci_margin <- qt(0.975, df = new_n - 1) * se_new
  lower <- mean_diff - ci_margin
  upper <- mean_diff + ci_margin
  return(c(lower, upper))
}

# Apply adjusted CI to each t-test result
ci1 <- adjust_ci(ttest1)
ci2 <- adjust_ci(ttest2)
ci3 <- adjust_ci(ttest3)
ci4 <- adjust_ci(ttest4)
ci5 <- adjust_ci(ttest5)
ci6 <- adjust_ci(ttest6)

ci1m <- adjust_ci(ttest1m)
ci2m <- adjust_ci(ttest2m)
ci3m <- adjust_ci(ttest3m)
ci4m <- adjust_ci(ttest4m)
ci5m <- adjust_ci(ttest5m)
ci6m <- adjust_ci(ttest6m)

# add single country CIs 'sCIs'
ame_all <- ame_all %>%
  mutate(upper_sCI = c(ci1[2], ci2[2], ci3[2], ci4[2], ci5[2], ci6[2], ci1m[2], ci2m[2], ci3m[2], ci4m[2], ci5m[2], ci6m[2]),  
         lower_sCI = c(ci1[1], ci2[1], ci3[1], ci4[1], ci5[1], ci6[1], ci1m[1], ci2m[1], ci3m[1], ci4m[1], ci5m[1], ci6m[1])   
)


```


##### Fig 1 new CIs

Makes no difference


```{r}

agg_png(here("Results", "Fig1_sCI.png"), res = 144, height = 750, width = 750)

ggplot(ame_all, aes(x = type, y = AME, fill = type)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.55, color = "black") +              # thinner bars
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), width = 0.15) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal12) +                    # 12 viridis colors
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Average Treatment Effect\nof Participation in ALE",
    fill = "Type"
  ) +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y  = element_text(hjust = 1, size = 8),
    axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 8),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 8),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

dev.off()

include_graphics(here("Results", "Fig1_sCI.png"))


```



### Population Predictions




#### Predictions

Many ideas supported by interactions with ChatGPT and Google Gemini

```{r}
# Get variable names as vectors
vars_raw    <- c("female", "FORBORN", "FORLANG", "children")
vars_within <- c("female_within", "forborn_within", "forlang_within", "children_within")
vars_between <- c("female_between", "forborn_between", "forlang_between", "children_between")

# Map within/between names used in the df
map_within <- c(female="female_within", FORBORN="forborn_within", FORLANG="forlang_within", children="children_within")
map_between <- c(female="female_between", FORBORN="forborn_between", FORLANG="forlang_between", children="children_between")

# Build counterfactual newdata for predict(m4) consistent with within/between coding
make_newdata_for_m4 <- function(df_base, spec, nfe_value) {
  stopifnot(all(names(spec) %in% vars_raw))
  nd <- df_base

  # Set NFE12 within directly to 0/1  (this is "within", so this is a strong intervention).

  nd$NFE12_within <- nfe_value - nd$NFE12_between

  # For each targeting var: if spec is 0/1, set within = target - between; else leave as observed (population mix).
  for (v in vars_raw) {
    target <- spec[[v]]
    if (!is.na(target)) {
      w <- map_within[[v]]
      b <- map_between[[v]]
      nd[[w]] <- target - nd[[b]]
    }
  }

  nd
}

# Compute AME for a spec: E[pred(NFE=1) - pred(NFE=0)] (weighted)
calc_ame_spec <- function(model_m4, df_base, spec, weight_var="aweight") {
  nd0 <- make_newdata_for_m4(df_base, spec, nfe_value = 0)
  nd1 <- make_newdata_for_m4(df_base, spec, nfe_value = 1)

  p0 <- predict(model_m4, newdata = nd0, re.form = NA)
  p1 <- predict(model_m4, newdata = nd1, re.form = NA)

  d  <- p1 - p0  # gain from ALE

  w <- df_base[[weight_var]]
  ok <- is.finite(d) & is.finite(w) & !is.na(w) & w > 0
  d <- d[ok]; w <- w[ok]

  ame <- weighted.mean(d, w)

  # Simple weighted SE (design-based would be better; this is a pragmatic approximation)
  se  <- sqrt( sum(w^2 * (d - ame)^2) / (sum(w)^2) )
  ci  <- ame + c(-1, 1) * 1.96 * se

  list(AME = ame, SE = se, lower = ci[1], upper = ci[2])
}

# Identify who is "in the targeted group" given a spec (raw vars and then NA means ignore)
in_group <- function(df, spec) {
  idx <- rep(TRUE, nrow(df))
  for (v in vars_raw) {
    target <- spec[[v]]
    if (!is.na(target)) idx <- idx & (df[[v]] == target)
  }
  idx
}

# Population gain per country from targeting spec:
# Boost skill_reading by AME for those in group AND NFE12==0, then take weighted mean difference.
# Population gain per country from targeting spec
calc_country_gains <- function(df2_full, spec, AME, ctx_data, weight_var="aweight") {
  
  df <- df2_full %>% 
    left_join(ctx_data, by = "iso3c") %>% 
    mutate(context_effect = replace_na(context_effect, 0))
  
  g  <- in_group(df, spec)
  g[is.na(g)] <- FALSE
  
  treatable <- g & df$NFE12 == 0 & !is.na(df$skill_reading)
  treatable[is.na(treatable)] <- FALSE
  
  df <- df %>%
    mutate(
      net_effect  = pmax(0, AME + context_effect),
      skill_boost = ifelse(treatable, skill_reading + net_effect, skill_reading),
      g_total     = ifelse(g, 1, 0),
      g_noale     = ifelse(g & NFE12 == 0, 1, 0)
    )
  
  df$g_noale[is.na(df$g_noale)] <- 0
  
  out <- df %>%
    filter(!is.na(iso3c)) %>%
    group_by(iso3c) %>%
    summarise(
      skill_base  = weighted.mean(skill_reading, .data[[weight_var]], na.rm = TRUE),
      skill_gain  = weighted.mean(skill_boost,   .data[[weight_var]], na.rm = TRUE) - skill_base,
      pct_group   = weighted.mean(g_total, .data[[weight_var]], na.rm = TRUE),
      pct_group_noale = weighted.mean(g_noale, .data[[weight_var]], na.rm = TRUE),
      group_skill_base = weighted.mean(skill_reading[g], .data[[weight_var]][g], na.rm = TRUE),
      pop18to65 = sum(SPFWT0, na.rm = TRUE),
      context_effect = first(context_effect),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
  
  out
}


```

#### Create all specifications

```{r}


make_all_specs <- function(vars = vars_raw) {
  # all subsets except empty
  subsets <- unlist(lapply(1:length(vars), function(k) combn(vars, k, simplify = FALSE)), recursive = FALSE)

  specs <- map_dfr(subsets, function(active) {
    grid <- expand.grid(rep(list(c(0,1)), length(active)))
    colnames(grid) <- active
    # inactive vars are NA ("all")
    for (v in setdiff(vars, active)) grid[[v]] <- NA_integer_
    grid %>% select(all_of(vars))
  })

  specs %>%
    mutate(
      spec_id = row_number(),
      spec_label = pmap_chr(across(all_of(vars)), function(female, FORBORN, FORLANG, children) {
        lab1 <- if (is.na(female))   "female=all"   else paste0("female=", female)
        lab2 <- if (is.na(FORBORN))  "FORBORN=all"  else paste0("FORBORN=", FORBORN)
        lab3 <- if (is.na(FORLANG))  "FORLANG=all"  else paste0("FORLANG=", FORLANG)
        lab4 <- if (is.na(children)) "children=all" else paste0("children=", children)
        paste(lab1, lab2, lab3, lab4, sep="; ")
      })
    )
}

specs_all <- make_all_specs()
nrow(specs_all)  # 80
```

#### ALE country effect

The 'human capital potential' is the fixed group-specific effect that occurs across countries. However, there is likely a context effect of ALE (the capacity for and effectiveness of ALE for example although the exact mechanism is not observed and it is only a context effect) that can be modelled by random-slopes. 

But I already have the group-specific results, based on a theoretical argument about a 'universal' potential of these groups. So I use these random-slopes for ALE as an offset, keeping the m4 slopes fixed.


```{r}
df2r$m4_baseline_pred <- predict(m4, re.form = NA)

m5 <- lmer(skill_reading ~ 0 + offset(m4_baseline_pred) + (1 + NFE12_within | iso3c), 
                      data = df2r)

# Extract random slopes for ALE
ctx_df <- ranef(m5)$iso3c %>% 
  rownames_to_column("iso3c") %>% 
  select(iso3c, context_effect = NFE12_within)

```

```{r}

df2r <- subset(df2r, !is.na(female) & !is.na(NFE12) & !is.na(children_within) &
                 !is.na(forborn_within) & !is.na(forlang_within) &
                 !is.na(ed3) & !is.na(gdppc) & !is.na(skill_reading))

ame_specs <- specs_all %>%
  mutate(res = pmap(select(., all_of(vars_raw)), \(female, FORBORN, FORLANG, children) {
    spec <- list(female=female, FORBORN=FORBORN, FORLANG=FORLANG, children=children)
    calc_ame_spec(m5, df2r, spec, weight_var="aweight")
  })) %>%
  mutate(
    AME   = map_dbl(res, "AME"),
    SE    = map_dbl(res, "SE"),
    lower = map_dbl(res, "lower"),
    upper = map_dbl(res, "upper")
  ) %>%
  select(-res)

# Example: pick the 12 "fully specified" 

ame_12 <- ame_specs %>%
  filter(if_all(all_of(vars_raw), ~ !is.na(.))) %>%
  mutate(type = spec_label)

# scale SE by sqrt(N_old / N_new); old N_old was the pooled micro N, but was using 5700.

adjust_se_to_n <- function(se, n_ref, n_new) se * sqrt(n_ref / n_new)

n_ref <- nrow(df2r)
n_new <- 5700

ame_12 <- ame_12 %>%
  mutate(
    SE_sCI = adjust_se_to_n(SE, n_ref, n_new),
    lower_sCI = AME - 1.96 * SE_sCI,
    upper_sCI = AME + 1.96 * SE_sCI
  )

```

#### Save for Shiny

```{r}
#saveRDS(df2,  here("Shiny", "shiny_df2.rds"))
#saveRDS(df2r, here("Shiny", "shiny_df2r.rds"))

# Save model
#saveRDS(m4,   here("Shiny", "shiny_m4.rds"))

# Save helper functions + mappings in one object
shiny_helpers <- list(
  vars_raw     = vars_raw,
  map_within   = map_within,
  map_between  = map_between,
  make_newdata_for_m4 = make_newdata_for_m4,
  in_group     = in_group,
  calc_ame_spec = calc_ame_spec,
  calc_country_gains = calc_country_gains
)

saveRDS(shiny_helpers, here("Shiny", "shiny_helpers.rds"))
```


#### Precompute  

The RAM needed to run predict every time is too large for shinyapps.io, this precomputes everything

```{r}
vars_raw <- c("female","FORBORN","FORLANG","children")

spec_grid <- expand.grid(
  female   = c(NA, 0L, 1L),
  FORBORN  = c(NA, 0L, 1L),
  FORLANG  = c(NA, 0L, 1L),
  children = c(NA, 0L, 1L)
)

spec_to_id <- function(row) {
  paste0(
    "f=", ifelse(is.na(row$female), "all", row$female), ";",
    "b=", ifelse(is.na(row$FORBORN), "all", row$FORBORN), ";",
    "l=", ifelse(is.na(row$FORLANG), "all", row$FORLANG), ";",
    "c=", ifelse(is.na(row$children), "all", row$children)
  )
}

spec_grid$spec_id <- apply(spec_grid, 1, function(x) spec_to_id(as.list(x)))


```



```{r}

precomp <- pmap_dfr(spec_grid, function(female, FORBORN, FORLANG, children, spec_id) {
  spec <- list(female=female, FORBORN=FORBORN, FORLANG=FORLANG, children=children)
  
  ame <- calc_ame_spec(m4, df2r, spec, weight_var="aweight")
  
  # PASS ctx_df INTO THE FUNCTION HERE
  cg  <- calc_country_gains(df2, spec, AME=ame$AME, ctx_data=ctx_df, weight_var="aweight")
  
  cg$spec_id <- spec_id
  cg$AME     <- ame$AME
  cg$SE      <- ame$SE
  cg$lower   <- ame$lower
  cg$upper   <- ame$upper
  cg
})

saveRDS(precomp, here("Shiny", "precomp_spec_country.rds"))
saveRDS(spec_grid, here("Shiny", "precomp_spec_grid.rds"))


```








## Citations

```{r}

packages %>%
  map(citation) %>%
  print(style = "text")

```


## Colophon

```{r}

sessionInfo()

```
